messages.content: You return valid JSON only. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.

You are an AI curriculum planner.

The backend has already retrieved textbook sections, resolved section relationships, and classified relationships by purpose.

Relationship meanings:
- DEPENDS_ON_UNIT: required ordering. to_section_id must be studied before from_section_id.
- TRANSFER_SUPPORTS_UNIT: optional support bridge only.
- RELATED_BY_CONCEPT: reinforcement/comparison only.
- next_steps: after-completion recommendations only.

Planning packet:
{"onboarding": {"subject": "physics", "topic": "I want to learn gravity", "current_level": "beginner", "confidence": "medium", "learning_goal": "Understand gravity and how it is used in physics problems.", "available_time": "2 hours", "preferred_learning_style": "clear explanations with worked examples", "deadline_or_pace": "steady pace"}, "learner_state": [], "prerequisite_check": {"asked": false, "answers": []}, "sections_by_id": {"ncert:chemistry:11:1:1.3": {"section_id": "ncert:chemistry:11:1:1.3", "chapter_id": "ncert:chemistry:11:1", "title": "PROPERTIES OF MATTER AND THEIR MEASUREMENT", "summary": "This section introduces the classification of matter's properties into physical and chemical categories and details the International System of Units (SI) used for quantitative measurements. It defines the seven fundamental base units, SI prefixes, and explains key physical quantities including mass, weight, volume, density, and temperature scales.", "role": "support"}, "ncert:physics:11:2:2.3": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:2:2.3", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:2", "title": "ACCELERATION", "summary": "This section defines acceleration as the rate of change of velocity with time, distinguishing between average and instantaneous acceleration. It explains how to interpret acceleration graphically using position-time and velocity-time graphs, and demonstrates that the area under a velocity-time graph represents displacement.", "role": "support"}, "ncert:physics:11:3:3.9": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:3:3.9", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:3", "title": "PROJECTILE MOTION", "summary": "This section introduces projectile motion as the combination of independent horizontal motion with constant velocity and vertical motion with constant acceleration due to gravity. It derives the parabolic trajectory equation and formulas for key parameters including time of flight, maximum height, and horizontal range.", "role": "support", "score": 2.0, "retrieval_reasons": ["transfer_support"], "matched_concept_ids": ["concept:horizontal_range_of_a_projectile", "concept:maximum_height_of_a_projectile", "concept:projectile_motion", "concept:time_of_flight", "concept:trajectory_of_a_projectile"]}, "ncert:physics:11:4:4.5": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:4:4.5", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:4", "title": "NEWTON’S SECOND LAW OF MOTION", "summary": "This section introduces Newton's second law of motion, defining linear momentum and establishing the quantitative relationship between net external force and acceleration. It explores the vector nature of force, its application to systems of particles, and introduces the concept of impulse.", "role": "support"}, "ncert:physics:11:6:6.8.2": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:6:6.8.2", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:6", "title": "Centre of gravity", "summary": "This section defines the centre of gravity (CG) as the point where the entire weight of a body acts and where the net gravitational torque is zero. It explores the relationship between the centre of gravity and the centre of mass, explaining that they coincide in a uniform gravitational field. Additionally, it details experimental methods to locate the CG and applies equilibrium conditions to solve mechanical prob...", "role": "target", "score": 9.0, "retrieval_reasons": ["key_term_match", "summary_match", "title_match"], "matched_concept_ids": []}, "ncert:physics:11:7:7.1": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.1", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:7", "title": "Introduction", "summary": "This section introduces the historical development of gravity and planetary motion theories. It covers Galileo's experiments on constant gravitational acceleration, the transition from the geocentric to the heliocentric model, and the observational work of Tycho Brahe and Johannes Kepler.", "role": "target", "score": 4.0, "retrieval_reasons": ["key_term_match", "summary_match"], "matched_concept_ids": []}, "ncert:physics:11:7:7.5": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.5", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:7", "title": "Acceleration Due to Gravity of the Earth", "summary": "This section explains how to calculate the gravitational force exerted by the Earth on objects located both outside and inside its volume using the shell theorem. It derives the formula for the acceleration due to gravity (g) at the Earth's surface and demonstrates how this value can be used to estimate the Earth's mass.", "role": "target", "score": 8.0, "retrieval_reasons": ["key_term_match", "summary_match", "title_match"], "matched_concept_ids": []}, "ncert:physics:11:7:7.6": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.6", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:7", "title": "Acceleration Due to Gravity Below and Above the Surface of Earth", "summary": "This section derives and analyzes the variation of acceleration due to gravity both above and below the Earth's surface. It demonstrates that gravity is at its maximum on the surface and decreases with both altitude and depth.", "role": "target", "score": 9.0, "retrieval_reasons": ["key_term_match", "summary_match", "title_match"], "matched_concept_ids": []}, "ncert:physics:11:7:7.8": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.8", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:7", "title": "Escape Speed", "summary": "This section defines and derives the escape speed of an object from a celestial body using the principle of conservation of energy. It calculates the escape speed for Earth and the Moon, discusses its implications for planetary atmospheres, and analyzes projectile motion between two spheres using the concept of a neutral point.", "role": "optional"}, "ncert:physics:11:7:7.9": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.9", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:7", "title": "Earth Satellites", "summary": "This section introduces Earth satellites, explaining their motion in circular or elliptical orbits governed by Kepler's laws. It derives the mathematical expressions for a satellite's orbital speed and time period, with special focus on satellites orbiting close to the Earth's surface.", "role": "optional"}, "ncert:physics:11:9:9.2": {"section_id": "ncert:physics:11:9:9.2", "chapter_id": "ncert:physics:11:9", "title": "Pressure", "summary": "This section introduces the concept of pressure as the normal force exerted per unit area, explaining its scalar nature, SI units, and behavior in static fluids. It also defines fluid density and relative density, illustrating these concepts with physical examples and data.", "role": "support"}}, "main_path_section_ids": ["ncert:physics:11:6:6.8.2", "ncert:physics:11:7:7.6", "ncert:physics:11:7:7.5", "ncert:physics:11:7:7.1"], "relationships": {"hard_dependencies": [], "parallel_support": [{"relationship_id": "rel:c805876071c2ad42d6ebcb77", "relationship_type": "TRANSFER_SUPPORTS_UNIT", "section_id": "ncert:physics:11:4:4.5", "source_target_section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.5", "bridge_concept_id": "concept:newtons_second_law_of_motion", "confidence": 0.95, "evidence_text": "Required concept evidence: Newton's second law (F = ma) is required to relate the gravitational force on a mass to its acceleration due to gravity (g). Teaching evidence: The rate of change of momentum of a body is directly proportional to the applied force...", "planning_meaning": "to_section can support from_section as an optional transfer bridge", "use_as": "optional support while studying the main module"}, {"relationship_id": "rel:b80d9148fcb85f45ef550c12", "relationship_type": "TRANSFER_SUPPORTS_UNIT", "section_id": "ncert:chemistry:11:1:1.3", "source_target_section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.5", "bridge_concept_id": "concept:density", "confidence": 0.9, "evidence_text": "Required concept evidence: The concept of uniform density is necessary to calculate the mass of the Earth's inner concentric spheres as a function of radius. Teaching evidence: Density is a physical property that relates the mass of a substance to its volum...", "planning_meaning": "to_section can support from_section as an optional transfer bridge", "use_as": "optional support while studying the main module"}, {"relationship_id": "rel:9ba69010261ff835bb7f683a", "relationship_type": "TRANSFER_SUPPORTS_UNIT", "section_id": "ncert:physics:11:9:9.2", "source_target_section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.5", "bridge_concept_id": "concept:density", "confidence": 0.9, "evidence_text": "Required concept evidence: The concept of uniform density is necessary to calculate the mass of the Earth's inner concentric spheres as a function of radius. Teaching evidence: Density rho for a fluid of mass m occupying volume V is defined as: rho = m/V", "planning_meaning": "to_section can support from_section as an optional transfer bridge", "use_as": "optional support while studying the main module"}, {"relationship_id": "rel:d92a4a4ba733db25e7f1b4d8", "relationship_type": "TRANSFER_SUPPORTS_UNIT", "section_id": "ncert:physics:11:2:2.3", "source_target_section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.1", "bridge_concept_id": "concept:acceleration", "confidence": 0.9, "evidence_text": "Required concept evidence: Required to understand Galileo's findings on the constant acceleration of falling bodies. Teaching evidence: Acceleration describes the rate at which the velocity of an object changes with time.", "planning_meaning": "to_section can support from_section as an optional transfer bridge", "use_as": "optional support while studying the main module"}], "reinforcement": [], "next_steps": [{"relationship_id": "rel:50752b9906a77eaf23ba1eb2", "relationship_type": "DEPENDS_ON_UNIT", "section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.8", "source_target_section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.5", "bridge_concept_id": "concept:acceleration_due_to_gravity", "confidence": 0.95, "evidence_text": "Required concept evidence: Rewriting the escape speed formula in terms of 'g' requires prior knowledge of the relation between the gravitational constant, planetary mass, and surface gravity. Teaching evidence: we obtain the standard formula for acceleratio...", "planning_meaning": "from_section can be suggested after completing to_section", "use_as": "recommended next section after completing the module"}, {"relationship_id": "rel:f5e8cee413cae5ddc96fa406", "relationship_type": "DEPENDS_ON_UNIT", "section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.9", "source_target_section_id": "ncert:physics:11:7:7.1", "bridge_concept_id": "concept:keplers_laws_of_planetary_motion", "confidence": 0.9, "evidence_text": "Required concept evidence: The section assumes prior knowledge of Kepler's laws to establish that satellite motion follows the same principles as planetary motion. Teaching evidence: Acceleration describes the rate at which the velocity of an object changes with time.", "planning_meaning": "to_section can support from_section as an optional transfer bridge", "use_as": "optional support while studying the main module"}]}, "relationship_policy": {"hard_dependencies": "DEPENDS_ON_UNIT edges must affect ordering; to_section_id should appear before from_section_id.", "required_concepts": "REQUIRES_CONCEPT explains prerequisite warnings and checkpoint questions.", "teaches_concepts": "TEACHES_CONCEPT explains concept coverage and can reveal downstream next steps.", "transfer_support": "TRANSFER_SUPPORTS_UNIT is an optional bridge, not mandatory curriculum sequence.", "related_by_concept": "RELATED_BY_CONCEPT is reinforcement or comparison only, not a prerequisite.", "next_steps": "Next-step paths are suggested after completion, not before the main module."}, "budget": {"estimated_chars": 14362, "target_chars": 25000, "hard_cap_chars": 40000, "trimmed": false}}

Now create the ordered curriculum module sequence.

Critical rules:
- Return JSON only.
- Use only section IDs present in planning_packet.
- Build required modules only from planning_packet.main_path_section_ids.
- Do not put optional support/reinforcement/next-step sections into source_section_ids.
- Use relationships.parallel_support only in parallel_support_section_ids or optional activities.
- Use relationships.reinforcement only in reinforcement_section_ids or review/practice recommendations.
- Use relationships.next_steps only in next_step_section_ids as after-completion suggestions.
- Respect DEPENDS_ON_UNIT hard dependency ordering.
- Use hard dependency evidence_reason to explain prerequisite warnings and ordering.
- Concepts are intentionally omitted from this first planner call except bridge_concept_id on section links.
- Do not produce concept IDs, activities, examples, exercises, milestones, detailed teaching content, or assessments.
- Make each module a coherent ordering unit for a later module-design LLM call.
- Put modules in the sequence the learner should follow.
- Explain why each module follows from the previous module and prepares the next module.
- Do not invent source ids, concept ids, examples, exercises, or relationships.

Required JSON shape:
{"modules": [{"module_id": "module:1", "title": "string", "module_goal": "string", "position": 1, "depends_on_module_ids": ["module:0"], "link_from_previous": "string", "link_to_next": "string", "source_section_ids": ["string"], "prerequisite_warnings": ["string"], "parallel_support_section_ids": ["string"], "reinforcement_section_ids": ["string"], "next_step_section_ids": ["string"]}]}
